creator: kbyanc@posi.net
text:
It would probably help if I actually attaced the port :)

 ~kbyanc@alcnet.com~
  FreeBSD - The Power To Serve - http://www.freebsd.org/
  Join Team FreeBSD - http://www.posi.net/freebsd/Team-FreeBSD/

# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	bgrab
#	bgrab/patches
#	bgrab/patches/patch-aa
#	bgrab/patches/patch-ab
#	bgrab/patches/patch-ac
#	bgrab/Makefile
#	bgrab/pkg
#	bgrab/pkg/COMMENT
#	bgrab/pkg/DESCR
#	bgrab/pkg/PLIST
#	bgrab/files
#	bgrab/files/md5
#	bgrab/files/Makefile
#
echo c - bgrab
mkdir -p bgrab > /dev/null 2>&1
echo c - bgrab/patches
mkdir -p bgrab/patches > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - bgrab/patches/patch-aa
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/patches/patch-aa << 'END-of-bgrab/patches/patch-aa'
X--- screen.h.orig	Sun Jan 24 03:24:50 1999
X+++ screen.h	Thu Sep 30 09:30:04 1999
X@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
X extern "C" {
X-#include <curses.h>
X+#include <ncurses.h>
X #include <stdio.h>
X }
X 
END-of-bgrab/patches/patch-aa
echo x - bgrab/patches/patch-ab
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/patches/patch-ab << 'END-of-bgrab/patches/patch-ab'
X--- main.cc.orig	Thu Sep 30 09:20:30 1999
X+++ main.cc	Thu Sep 30 09:22:51 1999
X@@ -7,7 +7,6 @@
X #include <sys/types.h>
X #include <sys/wait.h>
X #include <stdlib.h>
X-#include <curses.h>
X #include "server.h"
X #include "screen.h"
X #include "tree.h"
X@@ -15,7 +14,7 @@
X #include "grouplist.h"
X #include "findfile.h"
X 
X-#define		NNTP_PORT		119
X+unsigned NNTP_PORT = 119;
X 
X char *NNTP_SERVER = NULL;
X groupListPointer NNTP_GROUP = new groupList();
X@@ -249,6 +248,10 @@
X 	fprintf(stderr," -a user pass	Authenticate the login to the server with\n");
X 	fprintf(stderr,"		'user' as username and 'pass' as password.\n");
X 	fprintf(stderr,"		Both username and password must be present.\n");
X+	fprintf(stderr,"\nThe hostname or IP address of the news server can be\n");
X+	fprintf(stderr,"specified by the NNTPSERVER environment variable, or defaults\n");
X+	fprintf(stderr,"to \"news\". The port to connect to can be specified by the\n");
X+	fprintf(stderr,"NNTPPORT environment variable or defaults to 119.\n");
X 	exit(-1);
X }
X 
X@@ -301,6 +304,8 @@
X 
X 	if (getenv("NNTPSERVER")) NNTP_SERVER = strdup(getenv("NNTPSERVER"));
X 	else NNTP_SERVER = strdup("news");
X+
X+	if (getenv("NNTPPORT")) NNTP_PORT = atoi(getenv("NNTPPORT"));
X 
X 	process_command_line(argc, argv);
X 
END-of-bgrab/patches/patch-ab
echo x - bgrab/patches/patch-ac
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/patches/patch-ac << 'END-of-bgrab/patches/patch-ac'
X--- server.cc.orig      Thu Sep 16 16:50:51 1999
X+++ server.cc   Thu Sep 16 17:00:39 1999
X@@ -8,7 +8,10 @@
X server::server(char *host, unsigned long port) {
X	inbuf = 0;
X	bzero(buf,BUFSIZE+1);
X-	soc = connectto(host,port);
X+	if((soc = connectto(host,port)) == -1) {
X+	    fprintf(stderr, "Cannot connect to host \"%s\"\n", host);
X+	    exit(1);
X+	}
X }
X
X int server::sockfd() {
END-of-bgrab/patches/patch-ac
echo x - bgrab/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/Makefile << 'END-of-bgrab/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	bgrab
X# Version required:	1.2.3
X# Date created:		27 July 1999
X# Whom:			Kelly Yancey <kbyanc@posi.net>
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XDISTNAME=	bgrab
XPKGNAME=	bgrab-1.2.3
XCATEGORIES=	news
XMASTER_SITES=	http://thelamb.dhs.org/~rael/bgrab/ \
X		http://awwm.linuxberg.com/files/console/network/ \
X		http://powerlinux.linuxberg.com/files/console/network/
X
XMAINTAINER=	kbyanc@posi.net
X
XLIB_DEPENDS=	ncurses:${PORTSDIR}/devel/ncurses
X
XRUN_DEPENDS=	mmencode:${PORTSDIR}/mail/mmencode
X
Xpost-extract:
X	@${CP} ${FILESDIR}/Makefile ${WRKSRC}
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
END-of-bgrab/Makefile
echo c - bgrab/pkg
mkdir -p bgrab/pkg > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - bgrab/pkg/COMMENT
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/pkg/COMMENT << 'END-of-bgrab/pkg/COMMENT'
XRael's Binary Grabber - downloads binaries from newsgroups
END-of-bgrab/pkg/COMMENT
echo x - bgrab/pkg/DESCR
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/pkg/DESCR << 'END-of-bgrab/pkg/DESCR'
XThis is an automated binary extraction tool for UseNet newsgroups.
XIt will log into any newsgroup and download all the binary files. 
X
XWWW: http://thelamb.dhs.org/~rael/bgrab/
X
X  -Kelly
X   kbyanc@posi.net
END-of-bgrab/pkg/DESCR
echo x - bgrab/pkg/PLIST
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/pkg/PLIST << 'END-of-bgrab/pkg/PLIST'
Xbin/bgrab
X
END-of-bgrab/pkg/PLIST
echo c - bgrab/files
mkdir -p bgrab/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - bgrab/files/md5
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/files/md5 << 'END-of-bgrab/files/md5'
XMD5 (bgrab.tar.gz) = a59c008be5f134565b4ba00b5ae511fe
END-of-bgrab/files/md5
echo x - bgrab/files/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >bgrab/files/Makefile << 'END-of-bgrab/files/Makefile'
XOBJ	  =	main.o server.o screen.o tree.o multipart.o grouplist.o \
X		findfile.o
XGCC	 ?=	g++
XCPPFLAGS ?=	-O6 
XLIBS	  =	-lncurses
XPREFIX	 ?=	/usr/local
XDEST	  =	$(PREFIX)/bin
XINSTALL	 ?=	install
X
Xbgrab: $(OBJ) Makefile common.h
X	$(GCC) $(LIBS) -o bgrab $(OBJ)
X
Xclean:
X	rm -f $(OBJ) bgrab 
X
Xinstall: bgrab
X	$(INSTALL) bgrab $(DEST)/bgrab
X
Xall: bgrab
END-of-bgrab/files/Makefile
exit
